stem: The function f(t) = 28,000·2t/350 gives the number of bacteria in a population t minutes after an initial observation. How much time, in minutes, does it take for the number of bacteria in the population to double?
rationale: The expression 2t/350 doubles whenever the exponent t/350 increases by 1. So the bacteria population doubles whenever t increases by 350 minutes. Therefore the doubling time is 350 minutes.
